text: The surgeon begins the procedure with a caudal to cranial dissection of the mesentery, opening a mesenteric window. The inferior mesenteric vein is isolated, ligated with two hemoclips, and then cut. The inferior mesenteric artery is similarly ligated and cut.
The ureter is visible below the surgical field. A vascular plane is identified posterior to the rectum, and dissection continues along the lateral side. The surgeon incises the white line of Toldt.

The surgeon achieves complete mobilization of the left colon, encountering adhesions from previous surgeries. A suspicious area for endometriosis is identified, prompting a colonoscopy for confirmation.

The surgeon confirms the correct location and determines clean surgical margins for the procedure. The rectum is prepared for anastomosis, with the distal end transected using GA steppers. The left ureter is preserved, showing signs of fibrosis, and hemostasis is checked.
The surgeon performs an end-to-end colorectal anastomosis using a circular stapler, completing the connection between the rectum and colon.
